response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and backend software developer with roughly 16 years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application software — TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, and cloud-native tooling. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep hardware expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, SIMD/MIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design for deep-learning workloads. None of these domains appear anywhere in the candidate's resume. His AI-adjacent work (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) is software tooling, not silicon or accelerator optimization. The educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no graduate degree) combined with the absence of hardware or ML systems experience represents a fundamental mismatch with the role's core requirements.
strengths: Long tenure (16+ years) demonstrates sustained technical growth and professional reliability across diverse domains
Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, high-performance databases, millions of req/s) shows some performance engineering instinct
AI-adjacent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) indicates exposure to the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU workloads, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or deep-learning training/inference optimization
No graduate degree (Master's or PhD), and the equivalent-experience path would still require demonstrated hardware/accelerator expertise that is absent here

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload expertise
Strong general software engineering track record
